root_text.an10.86:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā mahākassapo rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.an10.86:1.2: Tatra kho āyasmā mahākassapo bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.an10.86:1.4: “Āvuso”ti kho te bhikkhū āyasmato mahākassapassa paccassosuṁ.
root_text.an10.86:2.2: ‘khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmī’ti.
root_text.an10.86:2.3: Tamenaṁ tathāgato vā tathāgatasāvako vā jhāyī samāpattikusalo paracittakusalo paracittapariyāyakusalo samanuyuñjati samanuggāhati samanubhāsati.
root_text.an10.86:2.4: So tathāgatena vā tathāgatasāvakena vā jhāyinā samāpattikusalena paracittakusalena paracittapariyāyakusalena samanuyuñjiyamāno samanuggāhiyamāno samanubhāsiyamāno irīṇaṁ āpajjati vicinaṁ āpajjati anayaṁ āpajjati byasanaṁ āpajjati anayabyasanaṁ āpajjati.
root_text.an10.86:3.1: Tamenaṁ tathāgato vā tathāgatasāvako vā jhāyī samāpattikusalo paracittakusalo paracittapariyāyakusalo evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca manasi karoti:
root_text.an10.86:3.3: khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmī’ti.
root_text.an10.86:4.1: Tamenaṁ tathāgato vā tathāgatasāvako vā jhāyī samāpattikusalo paracittakusalo paracittapariyāyakusalo evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāti:
root_text.an10.86:5.1: ‘Adhimāniko kho ayamāyasmā adhimānasacco, appatte pattasaññī, akate katasaññī, anadhigate adhigatasaññī.
root_text.an10.86:5.3: khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmī’ti.
root_text.an10.86:6.1: Tamenaṁ tathāgato vā tathāgatasāvako vā jhāyī samāpattikusalo paracittakusalo paracittapariyāyakusalo evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca manasi karoti:
root_text.an10.86:6.2: ‘kiṁ nu kho ayamāyasmā nissāya adhimāniko adhimānasacco, appatte pattasaññī, akate katasaññī, anadhigate adhigatasaññī.
root_text.an10.86:6.4: khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmī’ti.
root_text.an10.86:7.1: Tamenaṁ tathāgato vā tathāgatasāvako vā jhāyī samāpattikusalo paracittakusalo paracittapariyāyakusalo evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāti:
root_text.an10.86:8.1: ‘Bahussuto kho pana ayamāyasmā sutadharo sutasannicayo, ye te dhammā ādikalyāṇā majjhekalyāṇā pariyosānakalyāṇā sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ abhivadanti, tathārūpāssa dhammā bahussutā honti dhātā vacasā paricitā manasānupekkhitā diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhā.
root_text.an10.86:8.2: Tasmā ayamāyasmā adhimāniko adhimānasacco, appatte pattasaññī, akate katasaññī, anadhigate adhigatasaññī.
root_text.an10.86:8.4: khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmī’ti.
root_text.an10.86:9.1: Tamenaṁ tathāgato vā tathāgatasāvako vā jhāyī samāpattikusalo paracittakusalo paracittapariyāyakusalo evaṁ cetasā ceto paricca pajānāti:
root_text.an10.86:10.3: Abhijjhāpariyuṭṭhānaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.86:11.3: Byāpādapariyuṭṭhānaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.86:12.3: Thinamiddhapariyuṭṭhānaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.86:13.3: Uddhaccapariyuṭṭhānaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.86:14.3: Vicikicchāpariyuṭṭhānaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.86:15.1: Kammārāmo kho pana ayamāyasmā kammarato kammārāmataṁ anuyutto.
root_text.an10.86:15.2: Kammārāmatā kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.86:16.1: Bhassārāmo kho pana ayamāyasmā bhassarato bhassārāmataṁ anuyutto.
root_text.an10.86:16.2: Bhassārāmatā kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.86:17.1: Niddārāmo kho pana ayamāyasmā niddārato niddārāmataṁ anuyutto.
root_text.an10.86:17.2: Niddārāmatā kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.86:18.1: Saṅgaṇikārāmo kho pana ayamāyasmā saṅgaṇikarato saṅgaṇikārāmataṁ anuyutto.
root_text.an10.86:18.2: Saṅgaṇikārāmatā kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ.
root_text.an10.86:19.1: Sati kho pana ayamāyasmā uttari karaṇīye oramattakena visesādhigamena antarā vosānaṁ āpanno.
root_text.an10.86:19.2: Antarā vosānagamanaṁ kho pana tathāgatappavedite dhammavinaye parihānametaṁ’.
root_text.an10.86:20.1: So vatāvuso, bhikkhu ‘ime dasa dhamme appahāya imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti netaṁ ṭhānaṁ vijjati.
root_text.an10.86:20.2: So vatāvuso, bhikkhu ‘ime dasa dhamme pahāya imasmiṁ dhammavinaye vuddhiṁ virūḷhiṁ vepullaṁ āpajjissatī’ti ṭhānametaṁ vijjatī”ti.

translation_text.an10.86:1.1: At one time Venerable Mahākassapa was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.an10.86:2.2: ‘I understand: “Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.”’
translation_text.an10.86:2.3: They’re pursued, pressed, and grilled by the Realized One, or by one of his disciples who has the absorptions, and is skilled in attainments, in the minds of others, and in encompassing another’s mind.
translation_text.an10.86:2.4: Grilled in this way they get stuck or lose their way. They fall to ruin and disaster.
translation_text.an10.86:3.1: The Realized One or one of his disciples encompasses their mind and investigates:
translation_text.an10.86:3.2: ‘Why does this venerable declare enlightenment, saying,
translation_text.an10.86:3.3: “I understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’?”’
translation_text.an10.86:5.1: ‘This venerable overestimates themselves and takes that to be the truth. They perceive that they’ve attained what they haven’t attained, done what they haven’t done, and achieved what they haven’t achieved.
translation_text.an10.86:5.2: And they declare enlightenment out of overestimation:
translation_text.an10.86:5.3: “I understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”’
translation_text.an10.86:6.1: The Realized One or one of his disciples encompasses their mind and investigates:
translation_text.an10.86:6.2: ‘Why does this venerable overestimate themselves and take that to be the truth? Why do they perceive that they’ve attained what they haven’t attained, done what they haven’t done, and achieved what they haven’t achieved?
translation_text.an10.86:6.3: And why do they declare enlightenment out of overestimation:
translation_text.an10.86:6.4: “I understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”’
translation_text.an10.86:8.1: ‘This venerable is very learned, remembering and keeping what they’ve learned. These teachings are good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, meaningful and well-phrased, describing a spiritual practice that’s entirely full and pure. They are very learned in such teachings, remembering them, rehearsing them, mentally scrutinizing them, and penetrating them theoretically.
translation_text.an10.86:8.2: Therefore this venerable overestimates themselves and takes that to be the truth. …’
translation_text.an10.86:10.2: and often lives with a heart full covetousness.
translation_text.an10.86:10.3: Being full of covetousness means decline in the teaching and training proclaimed by the Realized One.
translation_text.an10.86:15.1: This venerable relishes work. They love it and like to relish it …
translation_text.an10.86:19.1: When there is still more to be done, this venerable stopped half-way after achieving some trifling distinction.
translation_text.an10.86:19.2: Stopping half-way means decline in the teaching and training proclaimed by the Realized One.’
translation_text.an10.86:20.1: It is quite impossible for a mendicant to achieve growth, improvement, or maturity in this teaching and training without giving up these ten qualities.
translation_text.an10.86:20.2: It is quite possible for a mendicant to achieve growth, improvement, or maturity in this teaching and training after giving up these ten qualities.”
